version_date: 18 November 2024 (publication and operational date; repeals SPP 3.7 Planning in Bushfire Prone Areas (2015))
scope_summary: State Planning Policy prepared under section 26 / Part 3 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 implementing risk-based land use planning for bushfire: avoid the risk first, otherwise manage and mitigate it to an acceptable level. It applies (with the Planning for Bushfire Guidelines and the Map of Bush Fire Prone Areas) to strategic planning proposals, structure plans, subdivision and development applications in designated bushfire prone areas that intensify development, increase people on site, or increase bushfire risk, with specific triggers tied to Bushfire Hazard Level, BAL ratings, lot size and vulnerable land uses. It sets a presumption against development in BAL-40/BAL-Flame Zone and provides an outcomes-based assessment pathway where acceptable solutions cannot be met.

key_numeric_standards.topic: presumption against development - radiant heat threshold
key_numeric_standards.unit: kW/m2 (radiant heat impact exceeding this = BAL-40 or BAL-Flame Zone)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: development sites or habitable buildings in bushfire prone areas

key_numeric_standards.topic: lot size trigger for development application requirements
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: single house or ancillary dwelling in a designated bushfire prone area above BAL-LOW without reticulated water (or BAL-40+ with reticulated water)
limited circumstances allowing a non-vulnerable habitable building in BAL-40/BAL-FZ

key_numeric_standards.topic: suitable destination separation from classified vegetation
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: evacuation destination relative to classified vegetation per AS 3959

key_numeric_standards.topic: suitable destination separation from Class G Grassland
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: evacuation destination relative to Class G grassland per AS 3959
Broader Landscape Assessment for intensification proposals

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (State Planning Policies) Regulations 2024
SPP 3.7 Planning in Bushfire Prone Areas (2015) (repealed)
Map of Bush Fire Prone Areas
Deemed Provisions - Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
Australian Standard 3959: Construction of Buildings in Bushfire-Prone Areas
